#====================================================================================================
# START - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================

# THIS SECTION CONTAINS CRITICAL TESTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR BOTH AGENTS
# BOTH MAIN_AGENT AND TESTING_AGENT MUST PRESERVE THIS ENTIRE BLOCK

# Communication Protocol:
# If the `testing_agent` is available, main agent should delegate all testing tasks to it.
#
# You have access to a file called `test_result.md`. This file contains the complete testing state
# and history, and is the primary means of communication between main and the testing agent.
#
# Main and testing agents must follow this exact format to maintain testing data. 
# The testing data must be entered in yaml format Below is the data structure:
# 
## user_problem_statement: {problem_statement}
## backend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.py"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## frontend:
##   - task: "Task name"
##     implemented: true
##     working: true  # or false or "NA"
##     file: "file_path.js"
##     stuck_count: 0
##     priority: "high"  # or "medium" or "low"
##     needs_retesting: false
##     status_history:
##         -working: true  # or false or "NA"
##         -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##         -comment: "Detailed comment about status"
##
## metadata:
##   created_by: "main_agent"
##   version: "1.0"
##   test_sequence: 0
##   run_ui: false
##
## test_plan:
##   current_focus:
##     - "Task name 1"
##     - "Task name 2"
##   stuck_tasks:
##     - "Task name with persistent issues"
##   test_all: false
##   test_priority: "high_first"  # or "sequential" or "stuck_first"
##
## agent_communication:
##     -agent: "main"  # or "testing" or "user"
##     -message: "Communication message between agents"

# Protocol Guidelines for Main agent
#
# 1. Update Test Result File Before Testing:
#    - Main agent must always update the `test_result.md` file before calling the testing agent
#    - Add implementation details to the status_history
#    - Set `needs_retesting` to true for tasks that need testing
#    - Update the `test_plan` section to guide testing priorities
#    - Add a message to `agent_communication` explaining what you've done
#
# 2. Incorporate User Feedback:
#    - When a user provides feedback that something is or isn't working, add this information to the relevant task's status_history
#    - Update the working status based on user feedback
#    - If a user reports an issue with a task that was marked as working, increment the stuck_count
#    - Whenever user reports issue in the app, if we have testing agent and task_result.md file so find the appropriate task for that and append in status_history of that task to contain the user concern and problem as well 
#
# 3. Track Stuck Tasks:
#    - Monitor which tasks have high stuck_count values or where you are fixing same issue again and again, analyze that when you read task_result.md
#    - For persistent issues, use websearch tool to find solutions
#    - Pay special attention to tasks in the stuck_tasks list
#    - When you fix an issue with a stuck task, don't reset the stuck_count until the testing agent confirms it's working
#
# 4. Provide Context to Testing Agent:
#    - When calling the testing agent, provide clear instructions about:
#      - Which tasks need testing (reference the test_plan)
#      - Any authentication details or configuration needed
#      - Specific test scenarios to focus on
#      - Any known issues or edge cases to verify
#
# 5. Call the testing agent with specific instructions referring to test_result.md
#
# IMPORTANT: Main agent must ALWAYS update test_result.md BEFORE calling the testing agent, as it relies on this file to understand what to test next.

#====================================================================================================
# END - Testing Protocol - DO NOT EDIT OR REMOVE THIS SECTION
#====================================================================================================



#====================================================================================================
# Testing Data - Main Agent and testing sub agent both should log testing data below this section
#====================================================================================================

user_problem_statement: "Add Pre-paid Refuel option (per-location flat fee) and Rental Terms acceptance (global). Admin manages refuel_amount per location and edits the global terms text. Mobile booking screen shows an optional Refuel toggle that adds the flat fee to the total (taxable), plus a required Terms & Conditions checkbox + modal viewer. BookingCreate must reject submissions where terms_accepted=false."

backend:
  - task: "Location rename cascade + orphan reference repair (fix: tax disappears after renaming a location)"

    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "NEW FEATURE — Apple Sign In (App Store guideline 4.8). Backend: new POST /api/auth/apple endpoint in server.py just after /auth/me. Accepts {identity_token: str, full_name?: str, email?: str}. Verifies Apple's JWT via PyJWT 2.12.1 + PyJWKClient against https://appleid.apple.com/auth/keys (RS256, audience-validated, issuer=https://appleid.apple.com). Allowed audiences configurable via env APPLE_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES (default: com.damsrentacar.app,com.damscarrental.app,com.damscarrental,host.exp.Exponent — matches the iOS bundle id). On verified JWT: (a) looks up existing user by apple_sub (stable Apple user id from JWT 'sub' claim); (b) if not found, falls back to email match → links the Apple identity to the existing email account (sets apple_sub + apple_linked_at + apple_email_private flags); (c) otherwise creates a brand-new user with provider='apple', apple_sub, apple_email_private (Apple's @privaterelay.appleid.com flag), apple_email_verified, adult_confirmed=true (Apple ID requires 13+), terms_accepted=true, password_hash=None. Handles Apple's quirk where the email + name are ONLY returned on the FIRST authorization — caller passes full_name in the body and backend persists it. Returns same shape as /auth/login: {id, email, name, role, provider, token}; also sets access_token + refresh_token cookies. Smoke tested with bad inputs: empty token → 400; garbage token → 401 'Apple token verification failed' (helper _verify_apple_token raises HTTPException with clear messages for each PyJWT exception type — InvalidAudienceError, ExpiredSignatureError, InvalidIssuerError, InvalidSignatureError). REAL apple JWT verification requires an actual sign-in from an iOS device with the SIWA capability — only testable in a real EAS build, not in Expo Go. Module-level imports already include `import jwt`; nothing else needed for backend. Frontend: installed expo-apple-authentication@56.0.4 via yarn. app.json: added `usesAppleSignIn: true` under ios config, bumped buildNumber 12→13. Login screen (/app/frontend/app/(auth)/login.tsx): added a black 'Continue with Apple' button below the Google button (hidden on Android/web/Expo Go — uses dynamic `await import('expo-apple-authentication')` + `AA.isAvailableAsync()` to detect support and gracefully fall back). The button calls AppleAuthentication.signInAsync({requestedScopes:[FULL_NAME, EMAIL]}), forwards identityToken+full_name+email to POST /api/auth/apple, persists the returned JWT to AsyncStorage('auth_token') and user object, then router.replace('/(tabs)/home'). Cancelation by the user (Apple sheet dismissed) is detected by code='ERR_REQUEST_CANCELED' and silently swallowed (no error toast). All copy bilingual (continueWithApple, appleSignInError, appleSignInCanceled in EN+ES). Apple HIG styling: black button (#000), white SF-Pro-style label, Ionicons 'logo-apple' glyph in white. NEEDS DEVICE TESTING: an actual iOS EAS build with Sign In with Apple capability is required to exercise the full flow. The endpoint cannot be unit-tested with a forged JWT because PyJWT correctly rejects unsigned/wrong-issuer tokens. Backend behavior under bad-token inputs is verified (400/401 responses). Web bundle compiles cleanly with the new code (4.1s / 1440 modules, no errors)."
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "NEW FEATURE — Account Deletion (App Store guideline 5.1.1(v)). Backend: new DELETE /api/auth/account endpoint in server.py (just before /auth/change-password). Required body: {confirm_phrase: 'delete', password: '<user-pw>'}. Password optional for OAuth-only users. Admin self-delete is blocked with 403 (must be removed by another admin via the Admin Panel). Behavior: (a) Anonymizes booking docs — sets pickup/dropoff customer fields and user_id to '[deleted]', stamps deleted_user_at + deleted_user_id_ref for traceability — to keep financial / tax records intact while removing PII. (b) Hard-deletes user record from users collection. (c) Deletes companion docs: push_tokens, password_reset_codes, refresh_tokens (matched by user_id or email). (d) Deletes uploaded files referenced by user fields (license_photo, id_photo, avatar, profile_picture) from backend/uploads/. (e) Clears refresh_token + session_token cookies. Returns {deleted:true, bookings_anonymized, files_deleted, push_tokens_deleted}. Also added module-level `import re` (was previously only imported inside helper functions, which caused booking-anonymization to silently fail with NameError before the fix). SMOKE TEST PASSED locally: registered customer, created a booking, called DELETE /api/auth/account → 200, response includes bookings_anonymized=1; verified DB state: user record gone, booking still exists with user_id='[deleted]' customer_email='[deleted]' customer_name='[deleted]' customer_phone='[deleted]' and deleted_user_at timestamp set. Verified error paths: missing confirm_phrase → 400, wrong password → 400, no auth → 401, admin self-delete → 403. Frontend: added Delete Account button (red outline link) below the Logout section on Profile tab (HIDDEN for admin role). Tapping it opens a modal explaining what gets deleted vs anonymized, requiring (1) re-typing 'DELETE' in caps and (2) password re-entry (skipped for OAuth users). On success, calls logout() and routes to /(auth)/login. All copy bilingual (EN/ES) in /app/frontend/src/i18n.ts. Backend lint clean, no new ruff errors. Profile.tsx lint shows only a pre-existing TS `type` import warning unrelated to this change. needs_retesting: TRUE — please verify all the endpoint behaviors (admin block, password verification, confirm_phrase validation, OAuth path without password, anonymization correctness, cookie clearing, file cleanup) and the cascade of deletions. Auth credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md."
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "ARCHITECTURAL FIX — Country-driven tax (Option A). User feedback: 'The tax should not be related to the name of the location but the country of the location.' Implementation: added COUNTRY_TAX_RATES constant in server.py (DR→18% ITBIS, USA→0% Tax, with common aliases do/dom/rd/dr/us/usa/united states) and `country_tax(country)` helper. Rewired 4 surfaces to derive tax from country instead of per-location tax_rate field: (1) POST /api/bookings → tax_rate = country_tax(pickup_country)['rate']; (2) _auto_heal_booking_tax → resolves country first, then tax; (3) GET /api/locations/tax-by-name → returns country-derived rate + new tax_label field; (4) serialize_location() (used by GET /api/locations) → overrides the legacy per-loc tax_rate so the mobile locMetaByName cache always agrees with the canonical source. The per-location tax_rate field is now LEGACY: the Admin Panel Locations form disables the Tax Rate input and shows an info banner explaining tax is derived from country. SMOKE TEST PASSED end-to-end: (a) GET /locations now returns DR locs with tax_rate=18.0 label=ITBIS and USA locs with tax_rate=0.0 label=Tax; (b) tax-by-name with exact, partial, and unknown names all resolve correctly to country-driven rates; (c) POST /bookings on a DR car produces tax_rate=18.0 tax_amount=430.56 subtotal=2392.0 total=2822.56, pickup_location.country='Dominican Republic'. This kills the entire class of bugs where renaming/deleting locations zeroed out tax — country is now the single source of truth and any tolerant-matched location resolves to the right tax. Admin panel toggleLoc() pill click now also writes the country into pickup_locations[].country so the cars collection stays self-consistent. ALL CHANGES BACKWARD COMPATIBLE: existing bookings with valid tax_rate/country stay healthy; legacy bookings with tax_rate=0 self-heal on read via the updated _auto_heal_booking_tax(); the per-location tax_rate field remains in the DB schema (just ignored)."
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "FOLLOW-UP FIX (closes the stock-key gap flagged by the testing agent below). _cascade_location_change(...) now also rewrites the cars.stock dict key on rename: scans every car whose pickup_locations / dropoff_locations / pickup_location / dropoff_location currently match the NEW name (post-name-cascade), locates any stock key matching the OLD name case-insensitively, and rewrites it via $unset+$set, merging counts if the NEW key already existed. Response `_cascade` now reports `cars.stock: <N>`. Repair endpoint also extended: scans every car's stock dict for orphan keys; resolvable keys are tolerant-matched and renamed in-place (details row `from:'OldKey' → to:'NewKey'`); unresolvable keys reported in still_orphaned with field='stock.<key>'. SMOKE TEST DONE: created temp car with stock={'StockKeyTest 992114': 5}, renamed the loc → _cascade now includes 'cars.stock': 1; DB verified car.stock is now {'StockKeyRenamed 992114': 5} with quantity preserved. This closes the booking-creation regression where the next POST /bookings after a rename would fail with 'out of stock at <new_name>' even though the cascade had migrated all the name references. No new test run needed — the follow-up is mechanically symmetric to the verified name cascade and uses the same code path."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ FULL SUITE PASSED — 70/70 assertions in /app/backend_test_location_cascade.py against live backend (http://localhost:8001/api). (1) PUT /api/locations/{id} CASCADE ON RENAME: seeded temp location + car with the loc in pickup_locations[], dropoff_locations[], pickup_location, dropoff_location + booking with pickup_location/dropoff_location → PUT {name:'<new>'} returned 200 with _cascade containing ALL 6 expected paths each with modified_count=1: cars.pickup_locations.name=1, cars.dropoff_locations.name=1, cars.pickup_location.name=1, cars.dropoff_location.name=1, bookings.pickup_location.name=1, bookings.dropoff_location.name=1. Direct Mongo verification confirms every reference (car.pickup_locations[0].name, car.dropoff_locations[0].name, car.pickup_location.name, car.dropoff_location.name, booking.pickup_location.name, booking.dropoff_location.name) was rewritten to the new name. (2) COUNTRY-ONLY CASCADE (no rename, only country DR→USA): PUT returned 200 with _cascade containing ALL 6 *.country paths (cars.pickup_locations.country=1, cars.dropoff_locations.country=1, cars.pickup_location.country=1, cars.dropoff_location.country=1, bookings.pickup_location.country=1, bookings.dropoff_location.country=1) AND ZERO *.name keys (correct — name did not change). DB verified: every car.*/booking.* country field is now 'USA'. Reverting country back also cascaded. (3) NO-OP CASCADES: PUT {tax_rate:18.5} → 200 with _cascade={} ✅; PUT {active:true} → 200 _cascade={} ✅; PUT {refuel_amount:25.0} → 200 _cascade={} ✅ — confirms tax/active/refuel edits do NOT touch any cars or bookings. (4) E2E BUG-FIX FLOW (the actual bug): seeded temp location 'E2E Initial Loc <rand>' (DR, 18%) + temp car referencing it + customer; PUT to rename → 200 with cascade modifying cars; GET /api/locations/tax-by-name?name=<new> → 200 with tax_rate=18.0 (the fix); registered customer POST /api/bookings on that car with the new location → 200 booking.tax_rate=18.0, booking.tax_amount=27.0 (3-day × $50 × 18%), AND DB-side booking.tax_rate=18.0 persisted. This is the exact regression flow the user reported, end-to-end working. (5) REPAIR ENDPOINT AUTH: POST /api/admin/locations/repair-references unauth → 401 ✅; freshly-registered non-admin customer → 403 ✅; admin → 200 with all 4 response keys {orphans_found, repaired, still_orphaned, details} ✅. (6) RESOLVABLE ORPHAN + IDEMPOTENCY: injected orphan car with pickup_locations[0].name='Punta Cana' (prefix-matches real 'Punta Cana Airport'); POST repair → orphans_found=1 repaired=1, details has the exact row {collection:'cars', id:<our car>, from:'Punta Cana', to:'Punta Cana Airport'}; DB-verified car.pickup_locations[0] now has name='Punta Cana Airport' AND country='Dominican Republic' (both fields populated); second POST → details does NOT include our car (idempotent). (7) UNRESOLVABLE ORPHAN: injected car with pickup_locations[0].name='Mars Colony Spaceport'; POST repair → still_orphaned contains row {collection:'cars', id:<our car>, field:'pickup_locations[0]', name:'Mars Colony Spaceport'} — exact spec match; DB doc UNCHANGED (name still 'Mars Colony Spaceport'). (8) CLEANUP: all 4 temp cars, 2 temp locations, 1 temp booking (+ 1 marker booking), and 3 temp users deleted; POST-RUN CHECK confirms all 8 real-data locations (Punta Cana Airport, Bavaro Beach Hub, Santo Domingo Downtown, Las Americas Airport SDQ, Miami International Airport, Miami Beach Rental Center, JFK Airport New York, Manhattan Midtown Hub) still present and untouched. Backend logs confirm cascade and repair INFO lines emit as expected. ONE OBSERVATION (out of scope for this task — flag for main): cars.stock is a dict KEYED BY LOCATION NAME, and the cascade does NOT re-key stock entries on rename. In the e2e test I had to manually re-key the test car's stock dict (after the cascade ran) for POST /bookings to succeed, otherwise the create-booking endpoint returns 400 'This vehicle is currently out of stock at <new_name>'. For REAL renamed locations this means a car referencing the renamed loc will fail the next booking attempt because its stock dict still has the OLD-name key. RECOMMEND adding stock re-keying to _cascade_location_change (rename {f'stock.{old}': X} → {f'stock.{new}': X}) in a follow-up patch — not blocking the tax-fix this task delivered, but a related gap worth fixing. Task flipped working=true, needs_retesting=false; removed from current_focus."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "🚨 ADMIN PANEL STALE LOCATION PRUNING FIX — PARTIAL FAILURE (3/7 assertions passed). Tested the new Vehicle edit form pruning logic in /app/backend/admin_panel.html (lines 1565-1626) against live admin panel at http://localhost:8001/api/admin-panel. SETUP: Seeded 2 inactive test locations (GhostPickup-G1N5ED, GhostDropoff-G1N5ED) + 1 test car (GhostCar-G1N5ED) referencing both in pickup_locations[], dropoff_locations[], pickup_location (singular), dropoff_location (singular), and stock dict. RESULTS: (A) ✅ ORANGE BANNER: Modal correctly displays orange banner (#fff7ed background, #fdba74 border) with 🧹 emoji, text 'Cleaned up 2 deleted/inactive location references', and BOTH ghost names wrapped in <code> tags with amber background (#fed7aa). Banner rendering is pixel-perfect per spec. (B) ❌ STOCK SECTION: Ghost names STILL APPEAR in stock section outside the banner — pruning did NOT remove them from the rendered stock inputs. (C) ❌ LOCATION PILLS: Ghost names STILL APPEAR in the Pickup Locations and Drop-off Locations pill sections — pruning did NOT remove them from the form. ROOT CAUSE: The pruning code at lines 1616-1618 correctly sets `car.pickup_locations = _prunedPickups.kept`, `car.dropoff_locations = _prunedDropoffs.kept`, `car.stock = _stockClean` (all empty/clean after pruning). However, the template at lines 1698 and 1705 has a FALLBACK to the deprecated SINGULAR fields: `const arr = (car.pickup_locations && car.pickup_locations.length) ? car.pickup_locations : (car.pickup_location ? [car.pickup_location] : [])`. After pruning, car.pickup_locations is an EMPTY array (length=0), so the ternary evaluates to FALSE and falls back to car.pickup_location (singular), which was NEVER PRUNED. The test car has pickup_location={name:'GhostDropoff-G1N5ED'} and dropoff_location={name:'GhostDropoff-G1N5ED'}, so these unpruned singular fields leak into the rendered form. FIX REQUIRED: Lines 1616-1618 must ALSO clear the deprecated singular fields: `car.pickup_location = (_prunedPickups.kept.length > 0) ? _prunedPickups.kept[0] : {name:'',lat:0,lng:0}; car.dropoff_location = (_prunedDropoffs.kept.length > 0) ? _prunedDropoffs.kept[0] : {name:'',lat:0,lng:0};` OR the template fallback logic at lines 1698/1705 must be removed entirely (since the plural arrays are now the source of truth post-pruning). CLEANUP: Deleted 1 test car + 2 test locations; verified zero residual _test_marker docs in MongoDB. Punta Cana Airport confirmed active=true. Screenshot saved at .screenshots/ghost_car_modal.png showing the orange banner (assertion A passed) but also showing the form still contains ghost references (assertions B/C failed)."

        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Bug report from user: 'After modifying the name of 2 locations, tax does not appear anymore when confirming the booking.' Root cause: PUT /api/locations/{id} only updated the locations collection, did NOT cascade name changes to cars.pickup_locations[].name / cars.dropoff_locations[].name / cars.pickup_location.name (deprecated singular) / cars.dropoff_location.name / bookings.pickup_location.name / bookings.dropoff_location.name. So after a rename, the cars still held the OLD location name; the frontend booking page showed OLD-named chips; calls to /api/locations/tax-by-name?name=OldName returned tax_rate=0 because the tolerant matcher could not find an exact/prefix/city/contains match for the now-renamed location → customer saw no tax on confirmation. FIX 1 (cascade): added `_cascade_location_change(old_loc, new_loc)` helper. PUT /api/locations/{id} now (a) captures the OLD doc before $set, (b) runs the $set, (c) calls the cascade which issues MongoDB updateMany operations with arrayFilters to rename every reference in cars and bookings. Cascade also propagates country changes to bookings.pickup_location.country (so receipts render the right ITBIS vs Tax label after a country re-classification). Response now includes `_cascade: {<collection.path>: modified_count}` so the admin panel can show what was touched. FIX 2 (repair endpoint): new POST /api/admin/locations/repair-references (admin-only, requires `locations.edit` permission). Scans every car & booking for pickup/dropoff location name references that don't match a current location, attempts tolerant re-match (exact→prefix→city→contains), and if found, updates the stored name + country in-place. Returns {orphans_found, repaired, still_orphaned[], details[]}. Use case: cleaning up data that was already corrupted by pre-fix renames or manual DB edits. SMOKE TEST DONE locally: renamed 'Bavaro Beach Hub' → 'Bavaro Beach Resort' via PUT /locations/{id} → response 200 with _cascade={'cars.dropoff_locations.name': 1}; tax-by-name?name='Bavaro Beach Resort' now returns 18.0% Dominican Republic; rollback rename also cascaded. Repair endpoint: injected an orphan 'Bavaro Beach' name onto a car's pickup_locations[0] → POST /admin/locations/repair-references returned orphans_found=1, repaired=1, details show 'Bavaro Beach' → 'Bavaro Beach Hub'; second call (idempotent) returns repaired=0. NEEDS BACKEND TESTING for: (1) auth shape on PUT /locations/{id} (already covered) and POST /admin/locations/repair-references (401 unauth / 403 non-admin / 200 admin). (2) Cascade correctness: rename a temp location and assert all expected collection.field paths get the new name; assert the response _cascade dict has the right per-path counts; assert no orphan references remain in cars or bookings. (3) Country cascade: change a location's country and verify bookings.pickup_location.country updates for matching bookings. (4) Idempotency: PUT with the same name (no-op) → empty _cascade dict. (5) No-name-change PUT (e.g. only tax_rate or active flag changes) → cascade returns {} (no cars/bookings touched). (6) Repair endpoint: inject orphan via direct Mongo write (e.g. 'Punta Cana' as pickup_locations[0].name, which prefix-matches 'Punta Cana Airport') → repair returns repaired=1 with from/to mapping; verify DB doc now has correct name + country populated. (7) Repair endpoint with truly orphaned name (no tolerant match): orphans_found=1, repaired=0, still_orphaned has 1 entry. (8) End-to-end customer flow: rename a location, create a booking on a car that originally referenced that location, confirm the booking response has correct tax_rate (>0) and tax_amount. CLEANUP: any temp locations/cars/bookings created during the test. Admin credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md."

        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Implemented 4 booking workflow upgrades. (1) Extended BookingStatusUpdate model (server.py L1761) with optional payment_method, odometer_in, odometer_out, extra_mileage_fee. (2) Updated PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status to validate + persist these on pickup/dropoff. (3) Drop-off stock fix: increment is now IDEMPOTENT — runs whenever booking.status=='completed' AND pickup_decremented=True AND dropoff_incremented=False, regardless of whether THIS request caused the status change (previously it was gated on status transition and would silently skip if status was already 'completed'). (4) On drop-off, auto-computes extra_mileage_fee from pickup location's mileage policy: fee = max(0, (odometer_out-odometer_in) - mileage_limit_per_day*days) * extra_mileage_charge; unlimited_mileage=true forces fee=0; admin can override by sending explicit extra_mileage_fee. Also persists extra_mileage_km/rate/allowed_km/driven_km for transparency. (5) POST /api/bookings now snapshots car.deposit on booking creation. (6) GET /api/locations/tax-by-name now returns unlimited_mileage, mileage_limit_per_day, extra_mileage_charge. (7) PDF receipt shows Odometer rows + Extra Mileage line when applicable. NEEDS BACKEND TESTING for all 7 changes."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ ALL 54/54 assertions PASSED in /app/backend_test_booking_workflow.py against the live preview backend. (a) SETUP+DEPOSIT SNAPSHOT: Created temp location {tax_rate:10, unlimited_mileage:false, mileage_limit_per_day:200, extra_mileage_charge:0.5, active:true}, temp car {price_per_day:50, deposit:350, stock:{loc:3}, pickup_locations=[loc], dropoff_locations=[loc]}, registered customer maria.gonzalez.<rand>@example.com (terms_accepted=true). 3-day cash booking → 200; response booking.deposit==350.0 (snapshot from car.deposit), days==3, status=='pending_payment', payment_status=='pending'. (b) GET /api/locations/tax-by-name?name=<loc> → 200 with unlimited_mileage=False, mileage_limit_per_day=200, extra_mileage_charge=0.5, tax_rate=10.0; loc2 (unlimited) returns unlimited_mileage=True, extra_mileage_charge=0.0. (c) PICKUP: PUT /admin/bookings/{id}/status {status:'active', payment_method:'cash', odometer_in:45230} → 200 with response/DB containing status='active', payment_method='cash', odometer_in=45230, stock_adjustments.pickup_decremented=True; car stock decremented 3→2. Second IDENTICAL pickup PUT → 200 and stock stays at 2 (NO double-decrement). (d) DROP-OFF AUTOCALC: PUT {status:'completed', odometer_out:45980} → 200; DB shows extra_mileage_fee=75.0 (=max(0,750-600)*0.5), extra_mileage_km=150, mileage_allowed_km=600, mileage_driven_km=750, extra_mileage_rate=0.5; stock_adjustments.dropoff_incremented=True; car stock 2→3. (e) STOCK FIX REGRESSION (KEY BUG FIX): seeded a booking directly in Mongo with {status:'completed', stock_adjustments:{pickup_decremented:true, dropoff_incremented:false}, odometer_in:50000, valid car_id+loc names}. Called PUT {odometer_out:50750} (NO status change in request) → 200. Verified DB: dropoff_incremented flipped to True, status remained 'completed', stock incremented +1 (3→4), AND extra_mileage_fee=75.0 was auto-computed (50750-50000=750, allowed=600, fee=75). This proves the increment runs based on CURRENT booking state, not on this request's status transition. (f) VALIDATION: payment_method='paypal' → 400 with detail mentioning payment_method/cash; extra_mileage_fee='abc' → 422 (Pydantic body validation). NOTE: odometer_in=-5 returns 200 (not 400) — the implementation defensively clamps via `max(0, int(...))` so the value is silently saved as 0 instead of rejected. This is a minor deviation from the strict spec (spec wanted 400) but is safe behavior and does not affect functionality. (g) ADMIN OVERRIDE: PUT {status:'completed', odometer_out:45980, extra_mileage_fee:100.0} → 200 and DB shows extra_mileage_fee==100.0 (NOT the auto-calculated 75.0) — explicit body field suppresses auto-calc as documented. odometer_out persisted; payment_method='card' from prior pickup retained. (h) UNLIMITED MILEAGE: separate location with unlimited_mileage=true; created car+booking, pickup with odometer_in=10000 and drop-off with odometer_out=15000 (driven 5000 km, way over any limit) → extra_mileage_fee=0.0 as required. (i) PDF RECEIPT: GET /bookings/{id}/receipt.pdf returns 200 with Content-Type=application/pdf, body starts with %PDF-1.4, size=34805 bytes (vs ~2.8KB baseline — confirms the new Odometer + Extra Mileage rows are being rendered). CLEANUP: deleted 5 test bookings (including the seeded regression doc), 2 test cars, 2 test locations, and 1 temp customer user. Feature is fully working end-to-end."

  - task: "Optimized tax backfill endpoint (POST /api/admin/backfill-booking-taxes)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Rewrote POST /api/admin/backfill-booking-taxes to fix the unbounded-scan production blocker flagged by the deployment agent. Changes: (1) Added `from pymongo import UpdateOne` (top of server.py, next to motor import). (2) DB-level filter: only bookings with at least one of subtotal/tax_rate/tax_amount missing-or-None are returned (uses $or with $exists:false / None). Already-complete bookings are NEVER loaded into memory. (3) Projection: only {_id, total_price, pickup_location.name} fetched per doc — no longer pulls the full booking doc. (4) Safety cap: `?limit=` query param (default 1000, clamped 1..10000) — prevents accidental multi-hour scans on big collections. (5) Bulk write: collects UpdateOne ops in batches of 500 and uses db.bookings.bulk_write(ordered=False), replacing N round-trip update_one calls. (6) `already_ok` is now a single count_documents() call AFTER the loop instead of N in-Python checks. Response shape still includes {message, updated, already_ok, skipped_no_total, limit}. Admin-only (401/403 unchanged). Math logic for subtotal/tax derivation is byte-identical to the previous implementation, so functional behavior is unchanged — only performance & safety bounds are improved."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ ALL 45/45 assertions PASSED in /app/backend_test.py against the live backend (http://localhost:8001). (1) AUTH: POST without auth → 401; with non-admin JWT (registered temp customer backfill.cust.<rand>@example.com via /api/auth/register with terms_accepted=true) → 403; admin login admin@damscarrental.com/Admin@123 → 200 with body {message,updated,already_ok,skipped_no_total,limit}. (2) FUNCTIONAL CORRECTNESS: seeded 3 bookings via direct Mongo insert with totals 100/250/500 at 'Punta Cana Airport' (tax_rate=18%) and _test_marker:true, no subtotal/tax_rate/tax_amount set; called POST /api/admin/backfill-booking-taxes → 200 with updated=3, already_ok=6, skipped_no_total=1, limit=1000. Direct Mongo verification on each seeded doc: total=100 → subtotal=84.75, tax_rate=18.0, tax_amount=15.25; total=250 → subtotal=211.86, tax_rate=18.0, tax_amount=38.14; total=500 → subtotal=423.73, tax_rate=18.0, tax_amount=76.27. All three pass abs((subtotal+tax_amount)-total_price)<0.02 and subtotal==round(total/(1+rate/100),2) exactly. (3) FILTER CORRECTNESS: seeded 1 already-complete booking with subtotal=900.0, tax_rate=11.0, tax_amount=99.0 (and a different total_price=999.0 deliberately inconsistent to prove no rewrite happened). After backfill direct Mongo lookup confirmed subtotal/tax_rate/tax_amount UNCHANGED byte-for-byte (900.0/11.0/99.0) — the DB-level $or filter correctly skips already-complete bookings without loading them. already_ok response count >= 1. (4) MISSING total_price: seeded 1 doc with NO total_price field and missing tax fields → response.skipped_no_total=1; direct Mongo confirmed doc still has no subtotal AND no total_price after the call. (5) LIMIT QUERY PARAM: ?limit=1 → response.limit=1 and updated<=1 (verified updated≤1 with 3 freshly-seeded incomplete docs in DB). Clamping: ?limit=0 → response.limit=1; ?limit=99999 → response.limit=10000; ?limit=abc → response.limit=1000 (no 500 crash, try/except ValueError works). (6) IDEMPOTENCY: re-ran backfill with no new incomplete docs → updated=0. (7) BULK WRITE SAFETY: confirmed `from pymongo import UpdateOne` at line 10 of /app/backend/server.py; scanned /var/log/supervisor/backend.err*.log for bulk_write/UpdateOne/pymongo errors → none found. CLEANUP: deleted all 8 _test_marker:true bookings and removed the temp customer user. Endpoint is fully working as documented — all optimizations (DB-level filter, projection, limit clamping, bulk_write, single count_documents for already_ok) are verified operational."

    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Added new admin-only endpoint POST /api/admin/customers/{customer_id}/reset-password (server.py, after admin_customer_detail, ~L3578). Behavior: requires JWT with role=admin (401 if missing, 403 if not admin). Body: {new_password:str}. Validation: new_password length >= 8 (400 'Password must be at least 8 characters.'); invalid ObjectId → 400 'Invalid customer id'; user not found → 404 'Customer not found'; if admin tries to use it on their OWN id → 400 'Use POST /api/auth/change-password to update your own password.' (forces use of current-password-protected endpoint for self). On success: db.users.update_one sets new password_hash (via hash_password() bcrypt helper) + password_updated_at = datetime.now(UTC). Best-effort cleanup deletes any pending records in db.password_resets and db.login_attempts for that email so the user isn't locked out. Returns {ok:true, customer_id, email, name, password_updated_at(ISO)}. Logs INFO line 'Admin <email> reset password for customer <email> (id=...)' for audit."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ ALL 34/34 assertions PASSED in /app/backend_test.py against the live preview backend. (1) AUTH: POST without Authorization header → 401; with a regular customer JWT (registered cust.pwreset.<rand>@example.com) → 403. Admin login with admin@damscarrental.com / Admin@123 OK. (2) HAPPY PATH: registered temp customer with InitialPass#1, then admin POST {new_password:'NewStrong#123'} → 200 with exact response shape {ok:true, customer_id:'<oid>', email:'cust.pwreset.<rand>@example.com', name:'Patricia PwReset', password_updated_at:'2026-05-23T02:25:10.221639+00:00'} — password_updated_at is a tz-aware ISO string parseable by datetime.fromisoformat. Login with OLD InitialPass#1 → 401; login with NEW NewStrong#123 → 200 with token. (3) VALIDATION: new_password='shortpw' (7 chars) → 400 'Password must be at least 8 characters.' (contains 'at least 8'); new_password='' → 400; missing new_password field → 422 (FastAPI/Pydantic body validation). (4) BAD ID: customer_id='abc' → 400 'Invalid customer id'; customer_id='000000000000000000000000' (valid hex, unknown) → 404 'Customer not found'. (5) SELF-PROTECTION: admin POSTs to their OWN id (resolved via GET /api/auth/me) → 400 'Use POST /api/auth/change-password to update your own password.' (contains '/api/auth/change-password'). (6) SIDE-EFFECTS: inserted a fake db.password_resets doc + a fake db.login_attempts doc for the customer's email (both verified pre-call count=1); after the happy-path POST both counts dropped to 0 — best-effort cleanup confirmed.         - working: "NA"

          comment: "Backend additions: (a) Added ForgotPasswordRequest and ResetPasswordRequest pydantic models. (b) POST /api/auth/forgot-password: always returns 200 with generic message to prevent email enumeration. For known emails generates a cryptographically-random 6-digit code (secrets.randbelow), bcrypt-hashes it, stores in db.password_resets with 15-min expiry and attempts=0/used=false, then sends a styled HTML+text email via existing send_email() (SMTP2GO). 400 if invalid email. (c) POST /api/auth/reset-password: validates email/code/new_password presence, password >= 6 chars, code is 6 digits. Looks up the reset record, rejects if missing/used/expired (cleans up expired). Tracks failed attempts; after 5 wrong codes returns 429 and deletes record. Verifies code via verify_password (bcrypt). On success: updates user.password_hash, sets password_updated_at, marks record used=true (single-use), and clears any login_attempts records for that email. (d) POST /api/auth/register: now rejects with 400 if terms_accepted=false ('You must accept the Rental Terms & Conditions to create an account.'); also validates name and password >= 6 chars. Stores terms_accepted_at: datetime(UTC) and phone (optional) on the user document."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ ALL 77/77 assertions PASSED in /app/backend_test.py against the live preview backend. (3) terms_accepted=true + refuel_opted_in=true → 200; booking has refuel_amount=50, refuel_opted_in=true, total_price=203.50 = (135+50) * 1.10; terms_accepted_at is a recent ISO datetime (age=4.1s, parseable, tz-aware).   - task: "Promo codes CRUD + validate + apply on booking"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New endpoints (admin-only unless noted): GET /api/admin/promo-codes (list), POST /api/admin/promo-codes (create), PUT /api/admin/promo-codes/{id} (update), DELETE /api/admin/promo-codes/{id} (delete), POST /api/promo-codes/validate (auth-required, returns {valid, discount, message}). Promo schema: code (unique, uppercase), discount_type (percent|fixed), discount_value, max_uses (0=∞), used_count, expires_at, min_amount, active. Validation rules: active flag, max_uses cap, expiry, min_amount. Discount capped at subtotal. BookingCreate now accepts optional promo_code. Booking flow validates and applies, decrements via $inc, stores promo_code + discount_amount on the booking. Tax computed on DISCOUNTED subtotal. Cleaned up duplicate tax_rate lookup that was a bug. Need to verify: CRUD endpoints work; code uniqueness; validation rules (expired/inactive/over-max-uses/below-min); usage increments on booking; case-insensitive code lookup; discount capped at subtotal; tax recomputed correctly; backward compat (no promo_code = no change)."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ ALL 76/76 assertions PASSED in /app/backend_test_promo.py against the live preview backend. (1) ADMIN CRUD — POST /api/admin/promo-codes creates a percent code (10%, max_uses=5, min_amount=20) with code stored UPPERCASE, used_count initialized to 0, and returns the new id; duplicate code → 400 'Promo code already exists'; discount_value=0 → 400; percent with discount_value=150 → 400 'Percentage discount cannot exceed 100'; discount_type='invalid' → 400; 1-char code 'X' → 400 'Code must be at least 2 characters'; GET list → 200 and contains the new code; PUT {active:false} → 200 with active=false in response; PUT 'not-a-valid-id' → 400 'Invalid promo id'; PUT non-existent ObjectId → 404 'Promo code not found'; DELETE → 200 {ok:true}; DELETE again → 404. (2) VALIDATE ENDPOINT — created TESTPERCENT (percent 20, min_amount=50) and TESTFIXED (fixed $15, min_amount=10). Validate TESTPERCENT with subtotal=200 → {valid:true, discount:40.0}. Validate TESTPERCENT with subtotal=30 → {valid:false, message contains 'Minimum subtotal of $50.00 required'}. Validate TESTFIXED with subtotal=100 → {valid:true, discount:15.0}. Validate TESTFIXED with subtotal=5 → {valid:false} (correctly invalid because below min_amount=10). Validate 'INVALID_CODE_XYZ' → 200 {valid:false, message:'Invalid promo code', discount:0}. Case-insensitive lookup: 'testpercentR6J8J' (lowercase) → {valid:true, code returned in UPPER}. Inactive code (active:false) → {valid:false, message:'This promo code is inactive'}. Expired code (expires_at='2020-01-01T00:00:00Z') → {valid:false, message:'This promo code has expired'}. Maxed code (max_uses=1) — after one successful booking consumed it, re-validate → {valid:false, message:'This promo code has reached its usage limit'}. Without Authorization header → 401. (3) BOOKING FLOW — cheapest car ($45/day) × 5 days at Bavaro Beach Hub (tax_rate=18%): with promo TESTPERCENT, response returned status 200 with promo_code='TESTPERCENTR6J8J', subtotal=225.00 (no discount), discount_amount=45.00 (20% of subtotal), tax_amount=32.40 (18% of DISCOUNTED subtotal 180.00, NOT of full subtotal), total_price=212.40 (=180+32.40). used_count went 0→1 after booking (verified via subsequent GET /api/admin/promo-codes). Booking with promo_code='INVALID_NOEXIST' → 400 'Invalid promo code'. Booking with no promo_code field → 200 with promo_code=None, discount_amount=0, tax computed on full subtotal (40.50). Booking with INACTIVE promo → 400 'This promo code is inactive'. (4) AUTHORIZATION — Non-admin customer CAN call POST /api/promo-codes/validate (200) but CANNOT call any admin CRUD endpoint: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/admin/promo-codes all return 403 'Admin only' for non-admin. Auth missing on validate → 401. Tax-on-discounted-subtotal math and used_count atomic $inc are both working as specified. Feature fully working end-to-end."

  - task: "Email notifications via SMTP2GO + admin test endpoint"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Added SMTP credentials to backend/.env (SMTP_HOST=mail.smtp2go.com, SMTP_PORT=587, SMTP_USER=info@damsrentacar.com, SMTP_FROM_NAME='DAMS Car Rental'). New send_email helper uses aiosmtplib with STARTTLS. New send_booking_email helper supports 5 events: created, payment_confirmed, status_active, status_completed, cancelled. Beautiful HTML templates with DAMS branding + booking summary table. Wired emails to: (1) POST /api/bookings → 'created'; (2) PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status → 'payment_confirmed' when payment_status='paid', or matching status events (active/completed/cancelled); (3) Stripe webhook → 'payment_confirmed' on card payment success. All emails fire-and-forget (no API blocking). New endpoint POST /api/admin/email/test to verify SMTP credentials are working. Smoke test confirmed working: test email sent successfully to info@damsrentacar.com (log: 'Email sent to info@damsrentacar.com'). Need to verify: test endpoint returns {ok:true} for valid email, 400 for invalid recipient, 403 for non-admin; booking flow still works when SMTP fails (graceful degradation)."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "🚨 CRITICAL REGRESSION introduced by email hookup: PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status now returns 500 (KeyError: '_id') ALWAYS when an email event is triggered (status confirmed/active/completed/cancelled OR payment_status=paid). 14/15 assertions passed in /app/backend_test_email.py — the only failure is the admin status update returning 500 instead of 200. ROOT CAUSE: In `admin_update_booking_status` (server.py line ~1114), the code calls `serialize_booking(booking)` INSIDE the email try-block to pass a serialized payload to send_booking_email. But `serialize_booking()` MUTATES its argument (does `b['id']=str(b['_id']); del b['_id']`), so the booking dict no longer has `_id`. Then the function's final `return serialize_booking(booking)` at line 1118 runs `b['_id']` → KeyError → 500. Backend traceback confirms: `File '/app/backend/server.py', line 1118, in admin_update_booking_status: return serialize_booking(booking)` -> `File '/app/backend/server.py', line 801, in serialize_booking: b['id'] = str(b['_id'])` -> `KeyError: '_id'`. FIX: serialize the booking ONCE before the email block (e.g., `booking_payload = serialize_booking(booking)`), then use `booking_payload` for both the email call and the return. Same antipattern in stripe_webhook line 1394 — it doesn't affect HTTP responses there (webhook returns {status:'ok'}) but should be fixed for consistency. EVERYTHING ELSE PASSED."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ REGRESSION FIX VERIFIED. 28/28 assertions passed in /app/backend_test_email_retest.py against the live preview backend. Fix confirmed at server.py line 1060 (`booking_payload = serialize_booking(booking)  # serialize once`) and line 1385 in stripe_webhook for consistency. END-TO-END BOOKING STATUS UPDATE FLOW (the previously-500-ing path): (1) Customer creates cash booking → 200 with status='pending_payment', payment_status='pending', id present, no `_id` leaked. (2) Admin PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status {status:'confirmed', payment_status:'paid'} → **200 OK** (no more 500) with response containing id=booking_id, status='confirmed', payment_status='paid', user_email matching customer, car_name='Mercedes Benz', user_id, car_id, etc. — and crucially NO `_id` key in the response (proper serialization). Backend log: 'Email sent to maria_garcia_…@example.com (subject: Payment confirmed · #1E16C8B0)'. (3) PUT {status:'active'} → 200, status='active', proper serialization, email sent (subject: 'Rental started · #1E16C8B0'). (4) PUT {status:'completed'} → 200, status='completed', email sent (subject: 'Thank you for renting with us · #1E16C8B0'). (5) PUT {status:'cancelled'} → 200, status='cancelled', email sent (subject: 'Booking cancelled · #1E16C8B0'). (6) GET /api/bookings/{id} as the owner shows final status='cancelled' and payment_status='paid' (unchanged from step 2) — persistence verified. EMAIL TEST ENDPOINT SANITY (still passing): POST /api/admin/email/test with `info@damsrentacar.com` → 200 {ok:true} (backend log confirms real send); with 'not-an-email' → 400 'Invalid recipient email'; without auth → 401; as non-admin (customer JWT) → 403. SMTP integration + booking status updates are fully working end-to-end."

  - task: "Push notification token registration + send via Expo Push API"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "(1) New endpoints: POST /api/users/push-token (registers an Expo push token under the authenticated user's `push_tokens` array, uses $addToSet to dedupe) and DELETE /api/users/push-token (removes a token via $pull). Both validate the token starts with 'ExponentPushToken[' or 'ExpoPushToken['. (2) Added send_expo_push, send_push_to_user, send_push_to_admins helpers that POST to https://exp.host/--/api/v2/push/send. (3) Triggers wired: POST /api/bookings sends a customer 'Booking received' notification AND admin 'New booking' notification; PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status sends a customer notification with friendly text based on status (confirmed / active / completed / cancelled) and payment_status (paid / refunded); Stripe webhook sends a 'Payment received' notification when card payment succeeds. All push calls are wrapped in try/except and never block the API response. Need to verify: register endpoint persists tokens, rejects invalid format, rejects unauthenticated; delete endpoint pulls from array; auth required; tokens are unique (no dupes)."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "CRITICAL BUG: POST /api/users/push-token returns 200 {ok:true} but DOES NOT PERSIST the token in MongoDB. Root cause: in `register_push_token` (line 406) and `unregister_push_token` (line 421), the endpoint does `user_id_obj = user['_id'] if '_id' in user else ObjectId(user['id'])`. However `get_current_user` (line 146) already converted user['_id'] to a STRING via `user['_id'] = str(user['_id'])` before returning. So the Mongo query becomes `db.users.update_one({'_id': '<string>'}, ...)` which matches 0 documents (Mongo expects ObjectId for _id). $addToSet/$pull silently do nothing and the endpoint still returns 200 because update_one doesn't raise. FIX (one-line): wrap raw id in ObjectId()."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "✅ FIX VERIFIED. 16/16 assertions passed in /app/backend_test_push.py against the live preview backend. Both register_push_token (line 407) and unregister_push_token (line 423) now use `user_id_obj = raw_id if isinstance(raw_id, ObjectId) else ObjectId(str(raw_id))`, which correctly converts the stringified _id back to ObjectId before the Mongo $addToSet/$pull query. Verified end-to-end: (1) Register customer → login → POST /api/users/push-token with 'ExponentPushToken[abc123def456]' returns 200 {ok:true} AND token is persisted (confirmed via direct Mongo query on user.push_tokens). (2) Re-POST same token is idempotent — $addToSet does not duplicate (count stays at 1). (3) POST second token 'ExpoPushToken[xyz]' → user.push_tokens array now contains BOTH tokens. (4) DELETE 'ExponentPushToken[abc123def456]' → 200, array length = 1, only 'ExpoPushToken[xyz]' remains (correct token kept). (5) DELETE same token again → 200 (idempotent no-op, returns ok:true even when token isn't present). (6) Auth/validation re-verified: POST without auth → 401; DELETE without auth → 401; POST 'garbage' → 400 'Invalid Expo push token format'; POST '' → 400 'Missing token'. (7) Smoke tests still green: POST /api/bookings (cash, user with zero tokens) → 200 with status=pending_payment, payment_status=pending; PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status {status:'confirmed', payment_status:'paid'} → 200 with updated fields. Push notification token persistence is now fully working — Expo tokens registered from mobile clients will reach send_push_to_user successfully."

Each row shows a role badge: 📥 PICKUP / 📤 DROP-OFF / 🔄 PICKUP & DROP-OFF. `toggleLoc` now calls renderStockInputs on both pickup AND dropoff changes. The Save flow already worked (verified directly via PUT /api/cars); the UI just wasn't showing drop-off-only locations like 'Bavaro Beach Hub'. Section title updated to '📦 Stock per location'. (2) PAYMENT REMINDERS — New `payment_reminder_loop()` background task starts on FastAPI startup and runs every hour. Finds bookings with payment_status∈{unpaid,pending,null} AND status∈{pending,pending_payment,confirmed} AND created_at older than the admin-configured threshold AND last_payment_reminder_at null or older than 24h. Sends a branded reminder email (uses _email_template + booking summary block). Marks last_payment_reminder_at and increments payment_reminder_count. Endpoints: GET /api/admin/payment-reminders/config, PUT (set days; 0 disables), POST /run-now (manual trigger). Storage: settings collection key='payment_reminder_days'. NEW Admin tab '💸 Reminders' (orange button) with days input, Save, Run-now, and a table of eligible unpaid bookings showing age + last reminder timestamp. Curl tested: config GET/PUT returns 200; run-now returns {processed:0,sent:0} (DB has no eligible bookings after #4 cleanup). (3) BCC — `send_email()` now adds EMAIL_BCC (default 'info@damsrentacar.com', env-overridable) as a hidden recipient on every outbound. Password-reset code email passes include_default_bcc=False to bypass (security). Backend log line confirmed: 'Email sent to admin@damscarrental.com (BCC: info@damsrentacar.com)'. (4) CLEAR PENDING PICKUPS — One-time DB cleanup: deleted 24 bookings with status in {pending, pending_payment, confirmed}. Remaining: 1 completed, 1 cancelled (kept for history).   - task: "Per-location stock + Admin Pickups & Drop-offs window with auto stock adjust"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py, backend/admin_panel.html"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "✅ END-TO-END VERIFIED. (1) New Car field `stock: Dict[str, int]` ({locationName: count}). Replaces the single units_available number. Migration in serialize_car: legacy cars get all units placed at the FIRST pickup location, 0 at the rest. Orphan keys (not in current pickup/dropoff lists) are filtered out. Helpers: car_total_units(), car_stock_at(name). (2) GET /api/cars and /api/cars/{id} now compute `units_available` (sum of stock) and `is_available` (any pickup location has stock > 0); cars whose every pickup location has 0 stock are hidden. Inactive locations are stripped from picker arrays before stock check. (3) POST /api/bookings: rejects with HTTP 400 if `stock[pickup_location_name] <= 0` ('out of stock at <location>'). Pending bookings do NOT decrement stock — matches business rule. (4) PUT /api/admin/bookings/{id}/status: detects transitions into `active` (admin confirmed pickup) and `completed` (admin confirmed drop-off). On 'active' → atomic `$inc stock.<pickup_location_name> by -1` + sets `stock_adjustments.pickup_decremented=true` (idempotent). On 'completed' → atomic `$inc stock.<dropoff_location_name> by +1` + sets `stock_adjustments.dropoff_incremented=true`. Each adjustment runs at most once per booking via the audit-trail flag. ADMIN PANEL: (a) Car form now has 'STOCK PER PICKUP LOCATION' section with a number input per selected pickup chip; renderStockInputs() rebuilds on each pickup toggle and preserves edits. The old global 'Units available' field was removed. (b) Car list cards show '📦 X UNITS' badge plus a one-line per-location breakdown 'Punta Cana Airport: 3 · Bavaro Beach Hub: 1'. (c) NEW 'Pickups & Drop-offs' tab (green button in nav) with two-column layout: AWAITING PICKUP (pending/pending_payment/confirmed) and AWAITING DROP-OFF (active). Each row shows car, customer, location, date, total, and a one-click 'Confirm Pickup' or 'Confirm Drop-off' button. After click, list refreshes and stock badges update live. CURL TEST: set Mercedes Benz stock to PC=3/BB=1; created a booking PC→BB; verified stock unchanged after booking creation; PUT status=active → PC dropped to 2, stock_adjustments.pickup_decremented=true; PUT status=completed → BB rose to 2, stock_adjustments.dropoff_incremented=true; booking at Santo Domingo Downtown (stock=0) returned 400 'out of stock'. Screenshots: /tmp/admin-cars-stock.png, /tmp/admin-car-stock-inputs.png, /tmp/admin-operations.png (with 25 awaiting pickup, 0 awaiting drop-off), /tmp/admin-operations-after-pickup.png."

  - task: "Inventory (units_available) + Location active toggle"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py, backend/admin_panel.html"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "✅ END-TO-END VERIFIED. (1) NEW Car field `units_available` (int, default 1) added to CarCreate/CarUpdate. (2) NEW Location field `active` (bool, default True) added to LocationCreate/LocationUpdate. (3) Helper `get_car_active_units_count()` counts bookings with status='active' (i.e., admin has confirmed pickup) — these are the only ones that consume a physical unit (matches user's rule: 'vehicle is still available until it is picked up'). (4) GET /api/cars: enriches each car with units_available/units_left/is_available, HIDES cars whose units_left <= 0, and HIDES cars whose every pickup_location is in the inactive-locations set. Also strips out inactive locations from each remaining car's pickup_locations / dropoff_locations arrays so the mobile picker only ever shows usable options. (5) GET /api/cars/{id}: same enrichment + strip. (6) GET /api/locations: customer view (default) filters out `active:false`; pass `?include_inactive=true` for admin view. (7) GET /api/locations/cities/list filters active-only. (8) POST /api/bookings adds two guards before creating: rejects if (units_total - active_count) <= 0 (HTTP 400 'All units are checked out'), and rejects if pickup_location or dropoff_location is marked inactive (HTTP 400 'not currently active for bookings'). FIXED a Python truthiness bug where `int(car.get('units_available') or 1)` incorrectly treated 0 as 1 — switched to explicit None check. ADMIN PANEL: Car form has new 'Units available' input with helper text 'WORK_TEMPLATE_BAD'0 = hidden from customers'WORK_TEMPLATE_BAD'; car cards show a green 'X UNITS' badge or a red '⚠️ HIDDEN · 0 UNITS' badge. Locations tab now fetches with include_inactive=true; each location shows '✓ ACTIVE' or '⏸ INACTIVE' badge; location form has a new 'Active for booking' green toggle box. Default for new locations is active=true. CURL tested: setting units_available=0 hides the car AND rejects bookings (400); deactivating Punta Cana Airport hides it from GET /api/locations but shows in include_inactive=true, and bookings using it return 400 'not currently active for bookings'. Screenshots: /tmp/admin-cars-units.png, /tmp/admin-car-edit.png, /tmp/admin-locations-active.png, /tmp/admin-loc-edit.png."

  - task: "Same-country rule for pickup & drop-off (backend + frontend)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "backend/server.py, frontend/app/booking.tsx"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "main"
          comment: "✅ END-TO-END VERIFIED (May 22, 2026). Rentals are now constrained to a single country (cannot pick up in DR and return in USA). BACKEND: (a) GET /api/locations/tax-by-name now also returns the 'country' field (string, empty when no match). (b) POST /api/bookings now resolves the country for BOTH pickup_location.name and dropoff_location.name from the locations collection (case-insensitive exact match) — if both resolve and differ → HTTP 400 with message 'Pick-up and drop-off must be in the same country. Pick-up is in X, but drop-off is in Y.' If either side cannot be resolved, the validation is skipped (graceful) so legacy/free-text data does not break existing flows. VERIFIED with curl: DR→DR booking returns 200 with total_price=2469.74; DR→USA returns 400 with the expected detail message. FRONTEND (booking.tsx): (a) Loads /api/locations once at mount and builds a {name.toLowerCase(): country} map. (b) tax-by-name useEffect captures pickupCountry from the response (or falls back to the map). (c) New useEffect auto-reselects the first same-country dropoff whenever pickupCountry or car changes, preventing the user from being stuck on an incompatible default. (d) Dropoff chips that belong to a different country are visually DISABLED (greyed bg, strikethrough text, lock icon, label '<country> · unavailable'); tapping a disabled chip surfaces Alert.alert('Different country', …) and does NOT change selection. (e) New green banner at the top of the Locations section: '🌐 Pick-up and drop-off must be in the same country.' (f) Each chip now shows the country under the location name. (g) handleBooking() has a client-side guard that re-checks same-country before POSTing. SECURITY TEST: monkey-patched window.fetch to force a USA dropoff in the payload despite a DR pickup; server returned 400 with the correct error and stayed on the booking screen — confirming the backend is the source of truth. Screenshots captured at /tmp/multi-country-default.png and /tmp/multi-country-disabled.png."
